title: Ace Of Spades
content:
Parable as metaphor - 
        profile in hard glint of light,
        buckskin garb
        merging from shadow &
        buckboards - 
        sandwiching of memory
        being elbowed
        thru a Deadwood City
        saloon door.

        Noneother.
        Dead Man's Hand.
        Cards strewn,
        last tumbler ...
        chamber on empty.
        Yancy Derringer modelling the
        latest revolver of his namesake,
        in pit & the palm
        bullet in the back
        for Wild Bill, just for a keepsake.

        Treasure-trove for the funeral parlour:
        "they done him up well". Peccadillo as provocation.
meaning:
Paul Cameron Brown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Ace Of Spades"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
